dialogues.text: Back in my day people helped each other. Spare $5, son?
dialogues.yes_response: Good kid. Your parents raised you right.
dialogues.no_response: Used to be different around here.

dialogues.text: Help me hang this flag on the wall? My ladder days are behind me. I'll give you $6.
dialogues.yes_response: Straight and proud, just like that. Good job.
dialogues.no_response: I'll ask the super. He owes me anyway.

dialogues.text: Bus pass ran out and I'm $3 short for a new one.
dialogues.yes_response: Thank you. Men like you keep the world turning.
dialogues.no_response: I'll walk. Did it for fifty years before buses existed.

dialogues.text: Take this package to the post office for me. My knees won't make it. $8.
dialogues.yes_response: Tell them it's prepaid. Just drop it at the counter.
dialogues.no_response: I'll mail it Monday. It's not urgent I suppose.

dialogues.text: Pharmacy won't cover my copay this month. $4 would do it.
dialogues.yes_response: I'll put your name in the book of good deeds.
dialogues.no_response: I'll skip a day. Won't kill me.

dialogues.text: Help me fix this bench — loose bolt. Won't take long. $5.
dialogues.yes_response: Wrench is in my coat pocket. You're a good man.
dialogues.no_response: I'll use tape for now. Works on everything.

dialogues.text: I'm owed $7 from the union but the check hasn't come. Lend it to me?
dialogues.yes_response: I'll repay it when it comes. You have my word.
dialogues.no_response: A man's word means nothing these days. Not your fault.

dialogues.text: Find my hearing aid in that garden bed over there. $6 if you find it.
dialogues.yes_response: Small tan thing. Can't hear a word without it.
dialogues.no_response: I'll get by reading lips like the old days.

dialogues.text: Just $2 for a newspaper. I like to read the obituaries. See who made it.
dialogues.yes_response: Ha. Thank you, son. I'll put in a good word for you.
dialogues.no_response: I'll read someone else's copy on the bench.

dialogues.text: Drive my grocery cart back to the store? My back's out. $7.
dialogues.yes_response: Just park it near the entrance. Here's the cart.
dialogues.no_response: I'll abandon it. I'm too old to care.
